Event details

item.title: Kristian Bugge & Ruthie Dornfeld – 2 Fiddle masters play Danish & American music and more!
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Kristian Bugge (Denmark) and Ruthie Dornfeld (Seattle) will entertain us with a wide range of music including Danish traditional fiddle tunes and music from the other Nordic countries to old-time American and self-composed original tunes\, spiced up with a few Venezuelan waltzes.\nKristian and Ruthie first met each other in the Danish folk music circles in the mid-1990’s\, where Ruthie had been touring regularly and growing a reputation as a well-renowned artist and fiddler since the early 1980’s. Later when Kristian was a music student\, Ruthie was guest-teaching at the Carl Nielsen Academy of Music in Odense\, Denmark and Kristian made sure to take the opportunity to study with her while she was there. About ten years later they met again in and around Seattle when Kristian had started touring and performing in the states. This summer marked a new chapter in their collaboration and friendship as they started touring and recording as a duo for the first time. Expect everything from wild polkas and jigs to lyrical waltzes\, fiery reels and happy hopsas\, all served with humor and stories from their many years on the road. The doors open at 7pm for the 7:30 cocnert in the phinney Community Hall (lower Brick Building). Advance tickets are $26/$24 members of SFS\, PNA\, or Skandia\, students\, or seniors 65+/ $10 Youth to age 17; at the door $2 more for adults ($28/$26/$10).

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Homebuyer Workshops
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Join us to learn the steps to home ownership and the resources that help pave the way! During part one\, we’ll cover the home buying process and best practices using recently sold homes as examples. Plus\, an informative Q&A with a home inspector. During part two\, we’ll dive deeper into available downpayment assistance (DPA) programs. You’re welcome to attend part one or both sessions. \nWe meet at 6pm every 2nd Monday of the month in the Phinney Center\, Blue Building. Snacks and NA drinks provided. Please RSVP at caitlin.lakere.com.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Jason Davis\, the Greenwood Branch Adult Librarian\, joins us to talk about all of the behind-the-scenes systems that keep Seattle’s libraries running smoothly. Come learn about all the steps that occur when you request a hold\, how interlibrary loans travel around\, how extensive the collection really is\, how books are selected for different branches\, what’s in the special collections\, and more! RSVP: 206.297.0875

item.title: PETS: Southwood Elementary School
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 1:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: Ain't Misbehavin'
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

The Fats Waller Music Show by Richard Maltby Jr. The Fats Waller Musical Show The club is alive, the band is swingin’, and the music of a jazz legend brings the house down. This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

From Taproot Theatre

This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: PETS: Park Orchard Elementary
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

From Taproot Theatre

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

From Taproot Theatre

Event details
